mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in your chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local environment to use the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from your cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment kagenti --namespace=kagenti

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command deletes the Kagenti deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade your Kagenti installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods --namespace=kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/kagenti.yaml

kubectl get pods --namespace=kagenti

```

> This command drains the nodes to prepare for upgrade.
> This command applies the latest deployment configuration.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods --namespace=kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure your cluster has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and verify your configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated to meet the demands of the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct roles assigned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI agent protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace=kagenti' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
